title: File upload flow with allow_upload, progress, and consume_uploaded_entries
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/photo_upload_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.PhotoUploadLive). The LiveView lets users upload up to 5 photos (.png, .jpg, .jpeg) with max_file_size: 5 * 1024 * 1024. In mount/3 call allow_upload(socket, :photos, accept: [".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg"], max_entries: 5, max_file_size: 5_242_880, progress: &handle_progress/3). Define handle_progress/3 that logs entry name on completion. Define handle_event("validate", _, socket) returning {:noreply, socket} (required for live validation). Define handle_event("save", _, socket) that calls consume_uploaded_entries(socket, :photos, fn %{path: path}, entry -> ... copy to uploads dir ... {:ok, final_path} end) and assigns the resulting paths to @uploaded_paths. The template must include <.live_file_input upload={@uploads.photos} />, progress bars using @uploads.photos.entries with :for, and cancel buttons using phx-click="cancel" phx-value-ref={entry.ref}. On cancel, handle_event("cancel", %{"ref" => ref}, socket) calls cancel_upload(socket, :photos, ref).
tier_rationale: The full upload API surface: allow_upload with 4 options + progress callback + live_file_input + consume_uploaded_entries + cancel_upload. Sonnet typically mixes old Plug.Upload patterns with new LiveView uploads or forgets the progress callback.
